svg.title: GridSearchCV cv=3 vs cross_val_predict cv=5 explanation
svg.desc: Two side-by-side diagrams showing how cv=3 inside GridSearchCV works for hyperparameter selection, and how outer_cv with n_splits=5 works inside cross_val_predict for honest OOF predictions.
svg.text: Goal: Find the best C value
Same 3 rounds run for C=1, C=10 etc.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does GridSearchCV pick the best C value?')

svg.text: ... rounds 4 and 5 follow the same pattern ...
Each sample appears in the test fold exactly once

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does oof_probs look like as an array?')
sendPrompt('Why is meta_train cheating-free because of OOF?')

svg.text: a model that trained on it

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why does GridSearchCV use only cv=3?')
sendPrompt('Why does cross_val_predict use cv=5 for stacking?')

svg.text: cv=3 scores stay inside the grid — discarded after selection
cv=5 result = oof_probs — stored in meta_train, actually used
One is a chooser, the other is an evaluator
